QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_6

question.text: A merchant banker with RBI primary dealer registration wants to accept public deposits. Is this permitted?

answer.text: Yes, primary dealers are allowed to accept public deposits
Yes, but only up to Rs. 100 crore
No, a merchant banker shall NOT accept or hold public deposits even if it has primary dealer registration
Yes, if SEBI grants a specific exemption

explanation.text: **The Logic:** While a merchant banker with primary/satellite dealer registration from RBI may carry on such dealer business, it shall NOT accept or hold public deposits. This is an absolute prohibition regardless of any other registrations held. **The Trap:** Since the MB is allowed to carry on primary dealer business, candidates might assume all aspects of that business — including deposit-taking — are permitted. But the deposit prohibition is carved out specifically. **Cross-Exam Context:** Restrictions on accepting deposits by intermediaries are also tested in NISM Series V-A and banking-related certifications. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** An MB's primary dealer role is limited to government securities trading — deposit-taking is a hard "no" under any circumstance.
